mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in real sales of final goods and housing starts signal synchronized demand expansion across consumer and capital goods sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over 60-day horizons, or positive returns occur in < 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs monetary support, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns during tightening cycles, or the 20-day forward return distribution shows no significant negative skew when the trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or positive returns occur in < 52% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained CPIAUCSL reading above 2.5% signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces equity valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP and real sales of final goods confirms broad economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates fragile supply chains that cannot sustain earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns during recovery phases, or 5-day forward returns show no significant negative bias when industrial production contracts.
